text: Artificial intelligence is increasingly common in offices and factories. One major use is automation, where AI handles repetitive tasks such as data entry or quality control, freeing employees for more complex work. Many productivity tools now incorporate AI, helping with scheduling, email sorting, and document analysis. However, the most effective approach is human-AI collaboration: humans provide creativity and judgment, while AI offers speed and data processing. This partnership inevitably leads to job changes—some roles may disappear, but new ones emerge, like AI trainers or ethics specialists. Employees who adapt by learning to work alongside AI often find their work becomes more interesting and less routine.
